name: 001_force_after_interactive_overwrites.sh
exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -f overwrites.
expected: Destination contains the source content after -i -f.

name: 002_interactive_after_force_decline_preserves.sh
exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -i prompts before overwrite.
expected: Answering no to the final interactive prompt leaves the destination unchanged.

name: 003_no_clobber_after_force_preserves.sh
exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -n does not overwrite an existing file.
expected: Destination remains unchanged under -f -n.

name: 004_force_after_no_clobber_overwrites.sh
exercises: If more than one of -i, -f, -n is specified, only the final one takes effect: final -f overrides -n.
expected: Destination is overwritten under -n -f.

name: 005_target_directory_places_source_inside.sh
exercises: -t, --target-directory=DIRECTORY moves all SOURCE arguments into DIRECTORY.
expected: The source file appears inside the target directory with its content.

name: 006_no_target_directory_existing_dir_fails.sh
exercises: -T, --no-target-directory treats DEST as a normal file; moving a file onto an existing directory as that file is an error.
expected: The command exits nonzero.

name: 007_t_and_T_conflict_fails.sh
exercises: -T no-target-directory and -t target-directory are mutually exclusive destination-selection modes.
expected: The command exits nonzero.

name: 008_update_none_preserves_existing_success.sh
exercises: --update=none is like --no-clobber: no destination files are replaced and skipped files do not induce failure.
expected: Existing destination content is preserved.

name: 009_update_none_fail_status_nonzero.sh
exercises: --update=none-fail skips existing destinations but diagnoses skipped files and induces a failure.
expected: The command exits nonzero when the destination already exists.

name: 010_update_older_replaces_when_dest_older.sh
exercises: --update=older replaces an existing destination file if it is older than the corresponding source file.
expected: Older destination is replaced with source content.

name: 011_update_older_preserves_when_dest_newer.sh
exercises: --update=older does not replace an existing destination file that is newer than the source file.
expected: Newer destination content is preserved.

name: 012_update_no_argument_defaults_older.sh
exercises: --update with no UPDATE argument defaults to older, not all.
expected: Bare --update preserves a destination newer than the source.

name: 013_update_all_replaces_even_newer.sh
exercises: --update=all replaces all existing files in the destination; this is also the default operation when --update is not specified.
expected: Destination is replaced even though it is newer than the source.

name: 014_backup_simple_creates_tilde.sh
exercises: --backup=simple makes a simple backup of each existing destination file using the usual '~' suffix.
expected: The simple backup file contains the old destination content.

name: 015_backup_numbered_creates_numbered.sh
exercises: --backup=numbered makes numbered backups.
expected: The first numbered backup dest.~1~ contains the old destination content.

name: 016_backup_existing_uses_numbered_if_present.sh
exercises: --backup=existing makes numbered backups if numbered backups already exist, simple otherwise.
expected: With dest.~1~ already present, the new backup is dest.~2~ containing the old destination content.

name: 017_backup_none_makes_no_backup.sh
exercises: --backup=none, also named off, never makes backups even if --backup is given.
expected: No simple backup file is created.

name: 018_suffix_overrides_backup_suffix.sh
exercises: -S, --suffix=SUFFIX overrides the usual backup suffix.
expected: The backup uses .bak and contains the old destination content.

name: 019_version_control_env_numbered.sh
exercises: The version control method may be selected through the VERSION_CONTROL environment variable when --backup is used.
expected: VERSION_CONTROL=numbered creates dest.~1~ containing the old destination content.

name: 020_simple_backup_suffix_env.sh
exercises: The backup suffix is '~' unless set with --suffix or SIMPLE_BACKUP_SUFFIX.
expected: The simple backup uses the .save suffix and contains the old destination content.

name: 021_strip_trailing_slashes_file_source.sh
stderr: mv: cannot move '/tmp/tmp.IBl590NaCM/src' to '/tmp/tmp.IBl590NaCM/dest': Not a directory
exercises: --strip-trailing-slashes removes any trailing slashes from each SOURCE argument.
expected: A file source named with a trailing slash is moved after stripping the slash.

name: 022_exchange_swaps_two_files.sh
exercises: --exchange exchanges source and destination.
expected: After exchange, the original destination content is at the source path.

name: 023_suffix_missing_argument_fails.sh
exercises: --suffix=SUFFIX has a mandatory argument; absent SUFFIX is an option error.
expected: The command exits nonzero when --suffix is missing its argument.
